quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in mid-air by flapping their wings in a figure-eight pattern up to 80 times per second. These tiny acrobats have excellent memories and can remember every flower they've visited and how long it takes each bloom to refill with nectar.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Garden Snails
short_read.teaser: These humble garden visitors have teeth on their tongues and can sleep for three years straight. Discover the surprising world of one of nature's slowest but most fascinating creatures.

medium_read.title: Where Orcas and Eagles Share the Waters
medium_read.teaser: The Pacific Northwest is home to one of the world's most remarkable wildlife spectacles where orcas hunt salmon while bald eagles soar overhead. This portrait captures a year in the life of the Salish Sea's most iconic residents.

long_read.title: Budapest: The Pearl of the Danube
long_read.teaser: From Roman ruins to Art Nouveau splendor, Hungary's capital tells a thousand-year story through its architecture, thermal baths, and resilient spirit. Travel through the layers of history that made Budapest one of Europe's most beautiful cities.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer Season
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Columbian black-tailed deer and their spotted fawns throughout the Olympic Peninsula. Early morning and evening are the best times to see mothers grazing with their young in meadows and forest edges. These gentle deer are smaller than their white-tailed cousins and have adapted beautifully to our coastal rainforest.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute when flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing, Barbara! You're absolutely right — that tiny heart is an incredible engine!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight — what an amazing little powerhouse!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are remarkable — they beat about 1,200 times per minute when flying! That's about 20 beats every second.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible metabolism, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in nectar each day and visit hundreds of flowers. At night, they enter a hibernation-like state called torpor to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm, Barbara! Perfect — you know your Hungarian well!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'köszönöm' (or the shorter 'köszi' among friends). 'Szia' means hello/bye, 'igen' means yes, and 'viszlát' means goodbye!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite different from most European languages. It's known for its complex grammar but beautifully expressive phrases for gratitude and connection.

puzzle_3.question: What do bald eagles typically build their nests from?
puzzle_3.correct_response: That's right! Bald eagles build massive stick nests that can weigh up to 2,000 pounds and be used for many years, growing larger each season.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Eagles build their nests from sticks and branches! These impressive structures can grow to 2,000 pounds over years of use as the eagles add more material each season.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The largest bald eagle nest ever recorded was in Florida — it was 9.5 feet wide and 20 feet deep! Pairs often return to the same nest year after year, adding new branches and strengthening their home.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
